question_text: What is the law of reflection?

options.label: A
options.text: The angle of incidence is always greater than the angle of reflection

options.label: B
options.text: The angle of incidence equals the angle of reflection

options.label: C
options.text: Light always reflects at 90 degrees to the surface

options.label: D
options.text: The angle of incidence equals twice the angle of reflection

correct_answer: The angle of incidence equals the angle of reflection
explanation: The law of reflection is a fundamental rule about how light behaves when it bounces off a surface. It states that the angle at which light hits a surface (measured from an imaginary line called the normal, which is perpendicular to the surface) equals the angle at which the light bounces away. Both angles are measured from this normal line.
